name: Wyhy
description_short: WyHy Federal Credit Union is a Wyoming-based, member-owned credit union serving state residents since 1953, offering competitive rates on savings, loans, mortgages, and business banking.
description_long:
WyHy Federal Credit Union has operated as a not-for-profit financial cooperative in Wyoming since 1953, serving Wyoming residents, professionals, worshippers, and students. As a credit union, it is member-owned rather than customer-based, meaning members participate in governance through voting and electing volunteer leadership. The institution is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. In 2025, Forbes ranked WyHy as the #1 Credit Union in Wyoming, recognizing its service quality and member trust.

WyHy offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts (Smart Returns Checking at 3.00% APY, FastTrac Savings at 0.30% APY, and Jumbo Term certificates at 3.70% APY), auto loans (rates as low as 4.99% APR for 2023+ vehicles, with recreational vehicle financing up to 9.05% APR), credit cards (Visa Platinum non-reward at 12.40% APY and rewards cards at 13.40% APY), personal loans (rates as low as 11.45% APY), mortgages (30-year fixed at 6.250% APR, 15-year at 5.500% APR), and home equity lines of credit (HELOC at 7.250% APR). The institution also provides business banking services and teen checking accounts.

WyHy distinguishes itself through its cooperative, member-owned structure that prioritizes member benefit over profit extraction. The institution actively promotes financial education through tools like a Security Center, Digital Banking Center, and Monday Messages educational content. Online banking enrollment and digital services are available for convenience. The credit union specifically welcomes all Wyoming residents and actively recruits through referral programs with rewards for successful member recruitment.

A key limitation is that WyHy's membership eligibility appears restricted to Wyoming residents or those with specific professional or religious affiliations with Wyoming organizations, making it unsuitable for out-of-state consumers. While the website highlights competitive rates, specific APR ranges for many products show variability depending on credit qualifications and other factors. The institution does not appear to offer traditional unsecured personal lines of credit or payday alternative loans at notably discounted rates compared to broader market options.
services: Recreational vehicle loans (7.55%-9.05% APR depending on loan amount)
Visa Platinum credit cards (non-reward and rewards options)
First mortgages (30-year at 6.250% APR, 15-year at 5.500% APR)
Home equity lines of credit/HELOC (from 7.250% APR)
Business banking and business loan services
Online banking platform with digital services
pros: Forbes-ranked #1 Credit Union in Wyoming (2025), indicating strong member satisfaction and service quality
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.99% APR for 2023+ model year vehicles with fresh start refinancing options
Tiered savings products including high-yield Smart Returns Checking at 3.00% APY and jumbo certificates at 3.70% APY
Comprehensive mortgage services with competitive rates (6.250% for 30-year fixed) and personalized support
Volunteer leadership governance allowing members to directly influence credit union direction through voting
Online banking and digital services available for account management convenience
cons: Membership eligibility restricted to Wyoming residents, professionals, worshippers, or students with Wyoming affiliations, limiting geographic accessibility
FastTrac Savings account offers only 0.30% APY, significantly below high-yield savings market rates
Credit card APY rates (12.40%-13.40%) are higher than many competitive rewards and non-rewards cards available elsewhere
Website does not provide clear information on minimum deposit requirements, monthly fees, or overdraft policies
Personal loan rates start at 11.45% APY, which may not be competitive for borrowers with excellent credit
best_for: Wyoming residents seeking member-owned financial institution with democratic governance and profit-sharing benefits
Vehicle buyers in Wyoming looking to refinance or purchase 2023+ model year cars at promotional rates
Wyoming homebuyers seeking personalized mortgage services with competitive rates and streamlined processes
Small business owners and entrepreneurs in Wyoming needing business banking accounts and financial resources
diagnosis: WyHy is ideal for Wyoming residents seeking a member-owned financial institution with strong service ratings, competitive auto and mortgage products, and governance participation. The primary caveat is geographic restriction to Wyoming membership eligibility, making it inaccessible to out-of-state consumers despite its otherwise comprehensive product lineup.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
